title: White House accuses Moonshot of distilling Anthropic’s Fable to build Kimi K3
excerpt: The model that rattled Silicon Valley last week now has Washington’s attention. A senior White House official has accused China’s Moonshot AI of breaking US export controls to build Kimi K3. It reached restricted Nvidia chips, he said, and leaned on American models to do it. Kratsios directs the White House Office of Science and […]
content_html:
The model that rattled Silicon Valley last week now has Washington’s attention. A senior White House official has accused China’s Moonshot AI of breaking US export controls to build Kimi K3. It reached restricted Nvidia chips, he said, and leaned on American models to do it.

Kratsios directs the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy. He made the claim in a post on X, Bloomberg reported. Moonshot “acquired GB300-equipped servers and has accessed GB300s in Thailand, likely to train its AI models,” he said. That breached both US export rules and the labs’ terms of service. The GB300 is part of Nvidia’s Blackwell line, which the US bars from sale to Chinese firms.

The accusation follows months of official unease. Trump officials have been examining whether firms used a loophole to ship advanced chips to Chinese subsidiaries abroad. A May memo reaffirmed the ban on selling Blackwells to Chinese companies.

The post went further on the software side. Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable model to build K3, Kratsios said. It ran a “sophisticated internal platform” for “large scale distillation against US models,” switching access methods to avoid detection. Distillation trains a cheaper model on a rival’s outputs. Kratsios drew a line: legitimate distillation is fair, covert theft of US technology is not. Nvidia and Moonshot did not respond to requests for comment.

Why Kimi K3 matters

Kimi K3 is the reason any of this landed. The open-weight model, released last week, quickly climbed the leaderboards. Moonshot says it trails only Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 and OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 on overall capability. Its arrival set off a rout in tech stocks, echoing DeepSeek a year earlier, and a wave of US alarm.

A fight already running hot

Kratsios was not alone. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told Fox Business that Washington could sanction Chinese labs for intellectual-property theft. The complaints echo long-running claims from OpenAI and Anthropic, which has accused Moonshot of distilling its models before.

“This administration supports open source models, but what we do not support is IP theft,” Bessent said, as Bloomberg reported. He later warned, in a post on X, that sanctions and entity-list designations “will be on the table.”

Nvidia’s dissent

As the worry spread, Nvidia chief executive Jensen Huang took the other side. He called open models like Kimi “excellent” and urged the US to embrace them, not ban them, Fortune reported. His case: shutting China out only pushes it to build its own stack. Z.AI has already done so, with a data centre running only Chinese chips.

The stakes

The timing bites. Moonshot is racing to close a funding round at up to $50bn. A Hong Kong listing could follow this year. Targeted US sanctions would not ban the model. Anyone can already download it. But they could keep American money away.

Kratsios published no evidence for his claims. The model itself is not in doubt. What has shifted is the argument: no longer whether China can compete, but how it got there.

sentences.en: What has shifted is the argument: no longer whether China can compete, but how it got there.

content_html_zh:
上周震惊硅谷的模式现在引起了华盛顿的关注。 一名白宫高级官员指责中国 Moonshot AI 违反美国出口管制来制造 Kimi K3。 他说，它使用了受限的英伟达芯片，并依靠美国型号来做到这一点。

克拉西奥斯领导白宫科学技术政策办公室。 据彭博社报道，他在 X 上的一篇帖子中提出了这一说法。 他说，Moonshot“购买了配备 GB300 的服务器，并在泰国访问了 GB300，可能是为了训练其人工智能模型。” 这违反了美国的出口规则和实验室的服务条款。 GB300 是 Nvidia Blackwell 系列的一部分，美国禁止向中国公司销售该产品。

这项指控是在官方数月的不安之后提出的。 特朗普官员一直在调查企业是否利用漏洞向中国海外子公司运送先进芯片。 五月份的一份备忘录重申了禁止向中国公司出售布莱克韦尔的禁令。

这篇文章在软件方面做了进一步的阐述。 Kratsios 表示，Moonshot 提炼了 Anthropic 的 Fable 模型来构建 K3。 它运行一个“复杂的内部平台”，用于“针对美国模式进行大规模提炼”，切换访问方法以避免被发现。 蒸馏在竞争对手的输出上训练更便宜的模型。 克拉西奥斯划定了一条界限：合法蒸馏是公平的，秘密盗窃美国技术则不然。 英伟达和 Moonshot 没有回应置评请求。

为什么 Kimi K3 很重要

Kimi K3 是这一切落地的原因。 上周发布的开放重量模型很快就登上了排行榜的榜首。 Moonshot 表示，其整体能力仅落后于 Anthropic 的 Claude Fable 5 和 OpenAI 的 GPT-5.6。 它的到来引发了科技股的暴跌，与一年前的 DeepSeek 相呼应，并引起了美国的恐慌。

战斗已经进入白热化阶段

Kratsios 并不孤单。 财政部长斯科特·贝森特告诉福克斯商业频道，华盛顿可能会因盗窃知识产权而制裁中国实验室。 这些投诉与 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的长期主张相呼应，后者此前曾指责 Moonshot 提炼其模型。

据彭博社报道，贝森特表示：“本届政府支持开源模式，但我们不支持知识产权盗窃。” 他后来在 X 上的一篇帖子中警告称，制裁和实体名单指定“将摆在桌面上”。

英伟达的异议

随着担忧蔓延，英伟达首席执行官黄仁勋站在了另一边。 据《财富》杂志报道，他称像基米这样的开放模式“非常出色”，并敦促美国拥抱它们，而不是禁止它们。 他的观点是：将中国拒之门外只会迫使它建立自己的堆栈。 Z.AI 已经这样做了，其数据中心只运行中国芯片。

赌注

时机紧迫。 Moonshot 正在竞相完成一轮高达 500 亿美元的融资。 今年可能会在香港上市。 美国有针对性的制裁不会禁止该模式。 任何人都可以下载它。 但他们可以把美国的钱拒之门外。

克拉齐奥斯没有发表任何证据来证明他的说法。 模型本身是没有疑问的。 争论的焦点已经发生了变化：不再是中国是否有竞争力，而是它是如何达到这一水平的。
